reasoning: I will pull the risk factors for type 2 diabetes from the provided text and present them as a concise bullet list for clarity.
output:
- Being over 45 years of age
- Being overweight or obese
- Having a first-degree relative (a parent, brother, or sister) with diabetes
- Belonging to certain racial/ethnic groups: African American, American Indian or Alaska Native, Asian American or Pacific Islander, or Hispanic American/Latino
- Having gestational diabetes, or giving birth to a baby weighing more than 9 pounds
- Having high blood pressure (≥140/90 mmHg)
- Having abnormal cholesterol levels — HDL cholesterol 35 mg/dL or lower, or triglycerides 250 mg/dL or higher
- Being inactive or exercising fewer than three times a week
- Having polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) (women only)
- On previous testing, having prediabetes (A1C 5.7%–6.4%), impaired glucose tolerance (IGT), or impaired fasting glucose (IFG)
- History of cardiovascular disease
